data.body:
COMPLETION VERIFICATION — VERDICT: FAIL (blocks close).

Scope verified: epic c81c (sulky-pane-flag), "plan-review container fan-out (G3/G4)" optimization. Verified against ticket state (show_ticket) and the repo working tree (read-only tools). Grounded findings:

PRIMARY ACCEPTANCE CRITERION UNMET — "All six child stories (S1-S6) closed."
Child status as read this session via show_ticket:
- S1 0250 (make-fawn-box, runner-seam caching): status=in_progress, signature=null.
- S2 c6e5 (pale-fist-prow, engine-wide caching): status=in_progress, signature=null.
- S3 ba7e (vale-noun-tumor, parallelize + warm-then-fan-out + budget cap): status=in_progress, signature=null.
- S4 98c6 (run-atom-drawl, merge G3+G4): status=OPEN, signature=null.
- S5 1762 (adept-pen-flame, child bin-packing): status=OPEN, signature=null.
- S6 da34 (safe-peg-tuner, fidelity-eval harness): status=in_progress, signature=null.
=> 0 of 6 children are closed (2 open, 4 in_progress); none carries a completion-verifier signature. The epic's own AC ("each child ... closed (each signed by the completion-verifier gate)" pattern) is decisively unmet. The epic itself is status=in_progress.

OTHER ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA — NOT DEMONSTRATED:
- "An 8-child epic review completes in minutes (was ~27 min) with materially lower token cost (cache reads non-zero)": no end-to-end measurement artifact is recorded as a closed deliverable; the de-risk experiments (E1/E2/A in S1/S3 comments) are SPIKES, not the epic-level acceptance measurement. Open eval-gated children S4/S5 are unstarted.
- "Findings remain within offline-eval tolerance ... (no fidelity regression)": the fidelity instrument is S6's live semantic eval, which is in_progress; S4/S5 are explicitly eval-gated on S6 and remain open. Two unresolved container-review G4 findings on S4 (per-child guardrail ownership split) are still live.
- "make lint + full suite pass": not verifiable as an epic-close artifact while the eval-gated work (S4/S5) is unimplemented.

NOTE (does not change the verdict): substantial S1/S3/S6 implementation HAS landed in the tree — runner.py:421 `_anthropic_cache_settings`; pass1.py warm-then-fan-out (lines ~156/402/415); src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-container.eval.yaml exists. But completion is determined by signed child-ticket CLOSURE, not code presence; closure status is authoritative and shows the work incomplete.

CONCLUSION: The epic does not meet its completion requirements. Closing it now would require --force-close (leaving it unsigned, the durable signal that validation did not pass). Recommended path: drive S1/S2/S3/S6 to signed close, implement+close the eval-gated S4/S5, then re-verify the epic-level wall-clock/token/fidelity/lint criteria.
